description: 알려진 엔티티 인스턴스 — 파이프라인 실행 시 자동으로 추가/업데이트된다.

properties.action_apr21: morning CNBC 'I don't want to' extend ceasefire; ~4pm ET Truth Social reversal: extends ceasefire indefinitely 'until such time as their proposal is submitted'; cited Iran 'seriously fractured' and Pakistan (Munir+Sharif) request; blockade maintained: 'continue the Blockade and, in all other respects, remain ready and able'

properties.action_apr22: IRGC seizes 3 ships in Hormuz (MSC-Francesca, Epaminondas, 3rd); Pezeshkian says 'open to deal' but accuses US of 'unconstructive signals'; dismisses Trump execution claims as 'false news'; IRGC escalates naval aggression despite ceasefire extension
2nd Washington talks with Lebanon confirmed Thursday Apr 24 (Huckabee joining); ceasefire fraying as Hezbollah continues attacks; continues joint war prep with US

seized MSC-Francesca (Panama, alleged Israel link) and Epaminondas (Liberia), fired on 3rd vessel in Hormuz; accused ships of AIS manipulation; UKMTO confirmed gunboat attacks off Oman coast; most aggressive naval action since blockade began

properties.status_apr22: IRGC seizes 3 ships, fires on all; Brent $101.91 (+3%) crossing $100 again; 'basically closed' (CNBC); dual blockade continues; Trump: Iran losing $500M/day; 6th consecutive day of major price swings

properties.day49_status: ceasefire day 10, Lebanon ceasefire day 1 (with violations), blockade day 5, Iran Hormuz 'open' but US blockade stays, 2nd round Monday Apr 21 confirmed, oil -11%

entities.name: UN Security Council Vote on Hormuz (Vetoed)

properties.targets: Tel Aviv, Haifa, Gulf oil facilities

entities.name: Tehran Severs Diplomatic Ties with US

properties.action_apr21: requested ceasefire extension from Trump (cited in Truth Social announcement); racing against time as Iran boycotts and Vance postpones
requested ceasefire extension from Trump alongside Sharif — cited by name in Trump Truth Social announcement; Pakistan's military-diplomatic dual track continues

properties.role: sole mediator in Iran war (WH confirmed)
properties.action_apr21: Munir + Sharif requested ceasefire extension from Trump — cited by Trump in extension announcement; racing against time as Iran boycotts talks

properties.action_apr17: claimed Hezbollah fight 'not over' despite ceasefire; publicly rebuked by Trump 'PROHIBITED from bombing'

properties.targets: Habshan UAE, Al Ruwais UAE, Sitrah Bahrain, Shuaiba Kuwait

entities.name: Iran Ballistic Missile Strikes on Israel (Apr 7)

properties.action_apr21: did NOT fly to Islamabad; trip 'postponed indefinitely' (Axios) as Iran boycotts 2nd round

properties.status_apr19: rift with IRGC deepens further; Vahidi identification as 'power behind the throne' confirms structural marginalization of diplomatic track

properties.action_apr21: adviser says: 'Trump's ceasefire extension means nothing, the losing side cannot dictate terms'

properties.action_apr22: continues firing on Israeli military sites day 2; ceasefire 'fraying'; both sides accuse other of violations; 200+ violations cited since ceasefire began

properties.status_apr22: ceasefire day 6, fraying; Hezbollah continues attacks; 2nd Washington talks Thursday Apr 24; Lebanon hopes for ceasefire extension beyond Apr 26 expiry

properties.context: Hours after Iran ceasefire declared, 'Operation Eternal Darkness', 'Black Wednesday'

properties.participants: US (Vance, Witkoff, Kushner), Iran (Araghchi, Ghalibaf ~70 people), Pakistan (host/mediator)
properties.sticking_points: nuclear (900 lbs enriched uranium), Hormuz control, frozen assets

properties.detail: Iran charges $1-2M per ship, Trump demands toll-free passage, 'serious disagreement' in Islamabad talks

properties.context: 40th day after Ali Khamenei death, declares 'new phase' in Hormuz management

properties.detail: $1/barrel oil toll in cryptocurrency

properties.vessels: USS Frank E. Peterson, USS Michael Murphy (Arleigh Burke-class)

properties.participants: Israel (Ambassador Yechiel Leiter), Lebanon (Ambassador Nada Mouawad), US (Secretary Marco Rubio, mediator)
properties.outcome: agreed to hold further talks at mutually agreed time and venue
properties.context: first direct talks since 1993, Hezbollah stepped up fire during talks

properties.items: Hormuz sovereignty, frozen assets ($6B), war reparations, region-wide ceasefire

properties.context: Iran demands release as negotiation precondition

properties.detail: Effective immediately, complete blockade of all ships entering/leaving Hormuz; interdict vessels that paid Iran tolls; begin mine clearing; 50% tariff on Iran-assisting countries

entities.name: Enriched Uranium Issue (900 lbs at Isfahan)

properties.detail: US demanded Iran hand over or destroy ~900 lbs (400kg) of near-bomb-grade enriched uranium at Isfahan; Iran refused; Trump: 'the only point that really mattered, NUCLEAR, was not'

properties.action: urged continued ceasefire, said Pakistan will facilitate new round of dialogue

properties.statement: gaps remain on several major issues

properties.detail: Trump threatens 50% tariff on any country assisting Iran, including potentially China

properties.scope: all maritime traffic to/from Iranian ports
properties.first_vessel: sanctioned tanker Elpis passed through; Ostria turned back

properties.detail: Araghchi claims US and Iran were 'inches away' from signing a memorandum of understanding. Clearest signal both sides came closer to formal agreement than publicly acknowledged. Blamed US 'maximalism, shifting goalposts, and blockade'.
US proposed Iran accept 20-year moratorium on all uranium enrichment and remove all HEU from country. Iran counter-offered 'monitored process of down-blending' existing stockpile.

properties.statement: As for the outcomes of this negotiation between Lebanon and the Israeli enemy, we are not interested in or concerned with them at all.
Called on Lebanon to cancel April 15 Washington meeting with Israel. Described talks as pointless.

properties.detail: Wafiq Safa: won't abide by any agreements. Naim Qassem: cancel April 15 talks. Over 2000 killed and 1M displaced in Lebanon since war. Hezbollah entered war firing missiles into Israel from March 2.
Hormuz faces dual blockade: Iran blocks free passage (tolls/mines) while US blocks Iran-bound traffic. Pre-war 135 ships/day → 4-10 ships/day. Near-total maritime paralysis.
UN FAO warns global food crisis could develop if normal Hormuz traffic doesn't resume. Substantial proportions of world farming products choked off. Largest oil supply disruption in history.

properties.statement: 'Highly probable' that peace talks will restart

properties.note: Physical crude still near $150/bbl (IEA), futures-physical disconnect widens

properties.assessment: largest energy security threat in history
properties.refinery_cuts: ME/Asia refineries cut 6 mb/d to 77.2 mb/d

properties.total_transits: at least 9 commercial vessels since Monday
properties.centcom_claim: no ships bound for Iran ports crossed blockade line

properties.detail: Trump told NY Post 'something could be happening over next two days'. NBC: talks could resume this week, possibly April 16. UN SG Guterres: 'highly probable'. Iran counter-offered 3-5 year moratorium vs US 20-year demand.
US waiver allowing countries to buy Russian crude expired, removing key supply source especially for India
Lebanese government and Hezbollah clash over Israel negotiations, sectarian tensions re-emerging

properties.action: issued first formal Red Sea blockade threat (Apr 15)
accompanied Munir to Tehran for mediation (Apr 15)

properties.detail: Trump told Fox Business war is 'very close to over' and stock market 'is going to boom'. Told NY Post talks could resume 'within two days' in Pakistan. Strongest language yet on war ending.
properties.context: Day 47 of conflict, day 8 of ceasefire, 6 days to expiry

properties.detail: CENTCOM declares blockade 'fully implemented' in less than 36 hours, completely halting Iran's economic sea trade. 10,000+ US personnel deployed. 6 merchant ships turned back. No ships made it past.

Khatam al-Anbiya Central HQ Commander Ali Abdollahi threatens to block ALL trade through Persian Gulf, Gulf of Oman, AND Red Sea unless US lifts blockade. First formal Red Sea blockade threat from Iran.
properties.significance: Qualitative escalation — extends conflict zone from Hormuz to global shipping lanes

properties.detail: Pakistan FM Asim Munir arrives in Tehran with Interior Min Mohsin Naqvi, carrying US message. Met FM Araghchi. Aim: persuade Iran to return to negotiating table for 2nd round.
properties.significance: Munir has rapport with Trump ('favorite field marshal') and knows IRGC leadership from intel service days

properties.detail: Iran government estimates total war losses. Demands reparations from US, Israel, and 5 Arab states (Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, Jordan) accused of allowing territory for attacks.
First intraday record since war began. S&P up 9.8% since Mar 30. Nasdaq +2%, Dow +0.7%. Market erased all war-related losses.
properties.driver: Trump 'war close to over' comments, 2nd round talks expectations

properties.trend: stable after previous day's -8% plunge, slight uptick
properties.context: Physical crude still near $150/bbl, futures-physical disconnect persists

properties.targets: US, Israel, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, UAE, Jordan
properties.basis: territory used for launching attacks on Iran

properties.detail: Iran's first formal threat to extend blockade beyond Hormuz to Red Sea and Gulf of Oman. Qualitative escalation from regional to global shipping disruption.

properties.statement: blockade 'as long as it takes', US 'maximally postured' to resume military operations, 'if Iran chooses poorly, bombs on infrastructure'

properties.role: Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff
properties.statement: blockade applies to ALL ships regardless of nationality, US will use force if ships don't comply, 13 ships turned back, contraband includes weapons/nuclear materials/oil/metals
ceasefire extension 'not true at this moment', Pakistan is 'the only mediator', 2nd round talks 'very likely'

properties.action: agreed to 10-day ceasefire with Israel, spoke with Trump, invited to White House

properties.statement: 'We will be respecting the ceasefire' on condition Israel stops all attacks and assassinations

properties.detail: 10-day ceasefire as Israeli 'gesture of goodwill'. May extend if progress demonstrated. Trump invites both leaders to White House. First ceasefire since 1983. Israel keeps 'security strip' in south Lebanon.
properties.hezbollah_stance: conditional acceptance via Moussawi — if Israel stops all attacks

properties.detail: Defense Secretary Hegseth: blockade 'as long as it takes'. US 'locked and loaded' for attacks on Iran energy facilities. 'If Iran chooses poorly, blockade AND bombs on infrastructure.' US 'maximally postured' to resume military operations.

Gen. Dan Caine: US military can intercept any Iran-linked ship worldwide, broadening beyond Hormuz. Contraband: weapons, nuclear materials, oil, metals, electronics. 13 ships turned back (up from 6). No boarding yet. Pacific operations targeting pre-blockade departures.

Field Marshal Munir visits Khatam al-Anbiya Central HQ. Received by Abdollahi. Iranian official says visit narrowed differences on nuclear/Hormuz/reparations. Pakistani sources report 'major breakthrough' on nuclear programme. Munir engaged both civil and military leadership.
properties.significance: Key mediator engaging Iran's military establishment directly

properties.detail: PM Sharif met Saudi Crown Prince MBS, discussed mediation and regional stability. Arrived Doha Apr 16. Met Qatar Minister. 4-day tour to align regional support for Hormuz reopening and Iran talks.
Oil dips below $100. Stocks rally on Lebanon ceasefire and Iran deal optimism. Physical crude still near $150.

White House formally identifies Pakistan as 'the only mediator' in US-Iran negotiations. Karoline Leavitt: 'many countries offered help but president wants to streamline through Pakistanis'. Dual track: Munir in Tehran (military), Sharif in Gulf (political).

Pakistani sources report 'major breakthrough' on Iran nuclear programme after Munir's Tehran visit. Iranian official says gaps narrowed on nuclear issue among 3 unresolved areas (nuclear, Hormuz, reparations). US 20-year vs Iran 3-5 year moratorium gap may be narrowing.

Araghchi: Hormuz 'completely open' for commercial vessels during ceasefire. Conditions: commercial only, no hostile country cargo, designated routes via Larak island, coordinated with Iran Ports and Maritime Organisation. Duration tied to ceasefire period.
properties.trump_response: Thanks but US blockade 'will remain in full force' until peace deal

properties.detail: IRGC-affiliated Fars News citing SNSC source: Iran will close Hormuz again if US blockade not lifted. IRGC considers US blockade ceasefire violation. Contradicts Araghchi's opening — reveals hardliner/pragmatist tension within Iran.
CNN: Iranian sources confirm next round Monday in Pakistan. Both delegations arriving over weekend. Trump: expects deal 'in coming days'. Coincides with ceasefire expiry.
properties.status_apr21: effectively collapsed — Iran boycotts ('waste of time'), Vance did not fly ('postponed indefinitely'), replaced by indefinite ceasefire extension

properties.detail: Lebanese army reports Israeli shelling of southern villages. Machinegun/artillery at ambulance team in Kunin (Nabatieh). 1 killed, 2 wounded in Kounine (motorcycle struck). Israeli military warns residents north of Litani.
After Netanyahu said Hezbollah fight 'not over', Trump publicly stated Israel 'PROHIBITED' from bombing Lebanon during ceasefire. Rare public rebuke of allied leader.
properties.significance: US enforcement of Lebanon ceasefire against own ally; signals Trump prioritizes deal over Israeli preferences

properties.note: Biggest single-day drop since war began. WTI lowest since Mar 10. S&P 500 +1.2% (weekly +4.5%), Nasdaq +1.5% (weekly +6.8%), both at new record highs.

properties.detail: Lebanese civilians celebrate first quiet day since March 2 after 6+ weeks of war. 2,196 killed, 1M+ displaced in Lebanon.
Sharif arrives last leg of Saudi-Qatar-Turkey tour. Bilateral with Erdogan. FMs of Turkey, Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Egypt meet on sidelines to discuss war and Hormuz blockade.

Pakistan-proposed: 4 countries + IAEA monitor Iran nuclear programme. Iran agreed in principle, conveyed acceptance to Munir. US not yet formally responded. Options for 440kg enriched uranium: send abroad to third party or downblend to 3%.

Iran's 'opening' has 3 conditions: (1) commercial vessels only, (2) cargo/ships not linked to hostile countries, (3) designated routes via Larak island (Iran's side). Only handful of ships actually transit — shipowners wary. Duration tied to ceasefire.

properties.statement: 'No negotiating with clenched fists'; Turkey working to extend ceasefire and maintain talks

properties.detail: IRGC declares Hormuz 'returned to previous state' under strict military control, reversing Araghchi's opening within 24 hours. Cited continued US blockade as ceasefire violation. IRGC warned 'no vessel should make any movement from anchorage' and approaching Hormuz 'will be considered cooperation with the enemy'.

entities.name: IRGC Fires on Ships in Hormuz (Apr 18)

properties.detail: Iranian gunboats fired on at least 2-3 commercial vessels including Indian-flagged VLCC Sanmar Herald despite prior clearance to transit. UKMTO confirmed attacks by IRGC fast boats. At least one ship hit with some damage, no injuries. India summoned Iran ambassador.
properties.vessels: VLCC Sanmar Herald (Indian-flagged), at least 1 other Indian ship

properties.detail: Axios/CNN scoop: US considering unfreezing $20B in Iranian assets in exchange for surrender of enriched uranium stockpile. 3-page MoU under discussion. Under compromise, some HEU shipped to third country, some down-blended in Iran under international monitoring. Iran has asked for sanctions relief north of $20B.
Secretary of State Rubio urged European countries to quickly reimpose sanctions on Iran, warning Iran violating agreement and nearing nuclear weapon capability.

India's foreign ministry summoned Iran's ambassador over 'serious incident' of firing on two India-flagged merchant ships in Hormuz, especially after Iran earlier let several India-bound ships through.
Israel 'shocked' by Trump's PROHIBITED warning, formally demands explanation from White House. Netanyahu government questions whether Lebanon is even included in the ceasefire.

Largely holding. Israeli bulldozers continue demolition and land-clearing in south. Shelling around Beit Lif, al-Qantara, Toul. Displaced Lebanese cautiously attempting to return. Israel maintains 'extensive security zone' to Syrian border.

IRGC-affiliated sources called Foreign Minister Araghchi an 'idiot' for opening Hormuz, then reversed his decision within 24 hours. Most dramatic public display of hardliner-pragmatist split in Iran's power structure during the war.

US offers $20B frozen assets release for Iran's enriched uranium. 3-page MoU. Options: HEU to third country or down-blend to 3% under monitoring. Supersedes/complements 20-year moratorium demand. Iran wants more than $20B.
Oil prices rose after Iran re-closed Hormuz, partially reversing Thursday's 11% crash. Market whipsawed by 24-hour open-close cycle.

properties.action_apr20: confirmed as controlling military junta; inserted Zolghadr into Islamabad delegation to monitor diplomats; blocking Pezeshkian; 'power behind the throne' confirmed by multiple outlets

properties.detail: USS Spruance fired on engine room of Iranian cargo ship 'Touska' after 6 hours of warnings. First US seizure of Iranian vessel since blockade began. Iran calls it 'armed piracy' and threatens military response.
IRNA: Iran won't participate in 2nd round due to 'excessive demands' and US blockade. But Trump sending Witkoff/Kushner to Islamabad anyway (Monday night). Vance skipping.
Trump accuses Iran of 'total violation' of ceasefire, threatens to destroy infrastructure. Linked to IRGC actions at Hormuz.
US global ship seizure operations expanding beyond Hormuz. Follow-on to Caine's Apr 16 global blockade expansion declaration. Multiple seizure operations worldwide targeting Iran-linked vessels.
Israel establishes 10km 'Yellow Line' military zone in southern Lebanon, 55 villages, razing homes 'like Gaza' during ceasefire day 3. De facto occupation expansion under ceasefire cover.

properties.note: Third major swing in 3 days (crash → rebound → surge)

properties.detail: Audio recording of Sanmar Herald crew: 'You gave me clearance' — then fired upon by IRGC. Second ship Jag Arnav also attacked. Evidence of IRGC arbitrary fire despite prior transit authorization.
Zero tanker transits on Sunday Apr 19 — first time in modern history that no tanker has passed through Hormuz. Complete paralysis of world's most critical energy chokepoint.
Trump threatens to destroy 'every single Power Plant and Bridge in Iran'. Linked to 'total violation' accusation. Most specific infrastructure targeting language since war began.
Ghalibaf: 'impossible for others to pass Hormuz while we cannot'. Acknowledges progress but says 'many gaps remain'. Partial alignment with IRGC line but with diplomatic reservation.

properties.action_apr22: says Tehran 'open to deal' but accuses US of 'unconstructive and contradictory signals'; cites 'deep historical mistrust'; blockade is main obstacle to 'genuine negotiations'; first moderate signal since IRGC/Ghalibaf 'waste of time'

properties.role: IRGC representative inserted into Iranian negotiating delegation
properties.action: inserted by Vahidi despite Ghalibaf/Araghchi protests; filed complaint that Araghchi exceeded mandate by showing flexibility on Axis of Resistance

properties.action_apr20: attended Trump emergency meeting on Iran
attended Trump emergency meeting on Iran

properties.detail: Trump reverses from 'highly unlikely' (Apr 20) and 'I don't want to' (morning CNBC Apr 21) to indefinite extension via Truth Social: 'until such time as their proposal is submitted'. Cited Iran 'seriously fractured' and Pakistan (Munir + Sharif) request. Blockade maintained: 'continue the Blockade and, in all other respects, remain ready and able.'
properties.significance: Averts Apr 22 ceasefire expiry and war resumption; but blockade stays, IRGC dismisses as 'ridiculous spectacle'

properties.detail: Axios: Vance did NOT fly to Islamabad; trip 'postponed indefinitely' as Iran boycotts 2nd round talks. Was scheduled to lead 300-member delegation for face-to-face 2nd round.
properties.significance: 2nd round of US-Iran talks effectively collapsed

entities.name: Iran Official Talk Refusal via Pakistani Intermediary (Apr 21)

properties.detail: Iran officially refuses talks through Pakistani intermediary: negotiations 'waste of time'. Three reasons: (1) ceasefire not covering Lebanon, (2) excessive demands, (3) naval blockade. Ghalibaf adviser: 'Trump's ceasefire extension means nothing, losing side cannot dictate terms.' FM: US blockade is 'act of war.'
properties.significance: Strongest rejection yet — escalation from Apr 19 'no plans' to Apr 21 formal refusal with stated reasons

entities.name: Hezbollah First Rocket Fire Since Ceasefire (Apr 21)

properties.detail: Hezbollah fires rockets at Rab al-Thalathine + drone toward Israel. First Hezbollah attack since Apr 16 ceasefire. Claims 220+ Israeli violations. Legal Agenda: 220 breaches Apr 17-19, 3 killed, 7 wounded.
properties.significance: Threshold crossing — transforms Lebanon ceasefire from 'violations' to 'active combat resumption'

properties.detail: Kan broadcaster: senior Israeli security official says Israel is 'jointly preparing with Washington for war's resumption'. Skepticism about Iran deal as driver.
properties.significance: First explicit report of joint war resumption planning during ceasefire period

properties.after_hours: WTI $88.60, Brent $94.89 on ceasefire extension news

properties.vessels: MSC-Francesca (Panama, alleged Israel link), Epaminondas (Liberia), 3rd vessel
properties.detail: IRGC fired on all 3 ships, seized 2 (MSC-Francesca and Epaminondas), redirected to Iranian coast. Accused of AIS manipulation and operating without authorization. UKMTO confirmed attacks. Hours after Trump ceasefire extension. Most aggressive IRGC naval action since blockade began.

Trump tells Fox News' Martha MacCallum: 'no time frame' for ending war; denies midterms driving decisions; says Iran losing $500M/day from blockade; 'THE BLOCKADE is absolutely destroying Iran'. Leavitt: 'no firm deadline' for Iranian proposal. US officials initially said 3-5 day window but Leavitt contradicted.

Iran President Pezeshkian says Tehran 'open to deal' but accuses US of 'unconstructive and contradictory signals'. Cites 'deep historical mistrust'. Blockade is main obstacle to 'genuine negotiations'. First moderate signal from Iran leadership since IRGC/Ghalibaf 'waste of time' rejection — reveals civil-military split.

properties.note: 6th consecutive day of major price swings. Brent re-crosses $100 threshold. Physical crude still much higher.
Equities and oil moving in opposite directions — stocks cheer ceasefire while oil fears supply disruption. Divergence is structurally significant.

properties.participants: Israel (Yechiel Leiter), Lebanon (Nada Moawad), US (Marco Rubio, Mike Huckabee, Michel Issa, Michael Needham)
properties.purpose: extend ceasefire beyond Apr 26 expiry, build on Apr 14 first round
properties.context: ceasefire fraying with Hezbollah attacks, 10-day truce expires Sunday

properties.action_apr22: joining US delegation for 2nd round Israel-Lebanon Washington talks Thursday Apr 24

properties.detail: Trump orders US Navy to 'shoot and kill' any boat laying mines in Hormuz. 'No hesitation.' Tripled mine-sweeping. Claims 'total control' of Hormuz, 'Sealed up Tight.' Iran's navy 'all 159 ships at bottom of the sea.'

Trump announces 3-week extension after 2nd round WH talks. Was set to expire Apr 26. Rubio leads with Huckabee, Needham, Issa. Lebanon asks for end to IDF demolitions, withdrawal, prisoner release. Netanyahu and Aoun invited to WH.
properties.participants: Israel (Leiter), Lebanon (Moawad), US (Rubio, Huckabee, Needham, Issa)

properties.detail: Israeli Channel 12 reports Ghalibaf resigned from negotiating team due to IRGC interference. Iran denies. Oil spiked to $107. Peace deal market drops to 9% YES.

properties.cause: shoot-to-kill order + Ghalibaf report + Hormuz disruption

properties.significance: IRGC fast-attack boat staging area near Hormuz; 33 boats detected by Sentinel-2 satellite Apr 22

properties.action_apr23: met PM Sharif to discuss peace efforts

properties.role: Potential replacement for Ghalibaf as negotiation lead
properties.note: Hardliner, former nuclear negotiator (2007-2013), former SNSC secretary. If confirmed, signals Iran negotiation stance hardening.

properties.statement: 'The ceasefire is meaningless in light of Israel's insistence on hostile acts, including assassinations, shelling, and gunfire'

properties.detail: 60-day deadline for congressional authorization of Iran war. Trump notified Congress Mar 2, deadline May 1. Must seek approval or certify 30-day withdrawal extension. Senate defeated WPR 5 times (52-47 party-line). Legal debate on whether ceasefire pauses the clock.

Hegseth: 'shoot to destroy' any ship laying mines, Iran navy 'gang of pirates with a flag,' blockade 'as long as it takes.' Criticized Europe/Asia for 'freeriding' on US Hormuz action. Gen. Caine: blockade fully operational. Joint press briefing at Pentagon.

Iranian FM Araghchi arrives Islamabad Friday night with small delegation. IRNA says visit is 'bilateral' (not for US meeting). Expected to go to Muscat then Moscow. Possible US meeting Monday if Araghchi stays. Represents de facto reversal of Iran's Apr 19 official talk refusal.

White House confirms Witkoff and Kushner heading to Pakistan Saturday morning for direct talks with Iranian counterparts, 'intermediated by Pakistanis.' Vance 'on standby' but not attending. Leavitt: 'move the ball forward towards a deal.' Iran denies direct meeting planned.
properties.participants: Steve Witkoff, Jared Kushner, Karoline Leavitt

properties.driver: Hegseth 'as long as it takes,' Ghalibaf resignation, Hormuz deadlock

properties.detail: Israel killed 6 Hezbollah fighters in exchange of fire in Bint Jbeil area. 2 killed in IDF airstrike on Touline (southern Lebanon). Hezbollah fired rockets at Shtula settlement. All within 24 hours of 3-week extension announcement. Hezbollah lawmaker Ali Fayyad called extension 'meaningless.'

Senate Republicans defeated bipartisan Iran War Powers Resolution for 5th time, 52-47 party-line vote. Failed to curb Trump's authority to conduct military operations against Iran. 60-day WPR deadline is May 1.

Trump abruptly cancelled Witkoff/Kushner trip to Pakistan for 2nd round Iran talks. Blamed Iran 'infighting' and said meeting delayed to Tuesday. 'We have all the cards.' 'Too much time wasted on traveling.' After cancellation Iran sent 'much better' paper within 10 minutes. Araghchi had already departed Islamabad for Oman.

entities.name: Netanyahu Orders Forceful Strikes on Hezbollah (Apr 25)

properties.detail: Netanyahu ordered IDF to strike Hezbollah targets in Lebanon 'with force' after Hezbollah fired 2 projectiles (1 intercepted, 1 open area) + explosive drones at IDF troops south of Forward Defense Line. Triggered sirens in Manara, Margaliot, Misgav Am. 2nd Hezbollah ceasefire violation. Israel told US to pressure Lebanon or risk ceasefire collapse.
properties.casualties_day9: 4 killed in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon

properties.detail: Iran FM Araghchi departed Islamabad Saturday afternoon after meeting Munir and Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi. Delivered Tehran's list of demands for ending war to Pakistani officials. Traveled to Muscat, then planned Moscow visit. 'No direct talks with US' maintained.

Iran President Pezeshkian said Tehran will not enter 'forced negotiations' while facing pressure and threats. Demanded Washington first remove 'operational obstacles, including the blockade' before talks. Reversal from Apr 22 'open to deal' signal. Reflects IRGC influence over presidential messaging.

Trump: 'They gave us a paper that should have been better and interestingly the minute I cancelled it, within 10 minutes, we got a new paper that was much better…they offered a lot but not enough.' Reiterated Iran cannot get nuclear weapon. Suggested future talks by telephone.
properties.significance: Cancellation as negotiation tactic; Iran responsive
